item.content:encoded:
This needs to reach 500 already, this is GLORIOUS.

What had the spot before this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like an asshole, because I was distinctly un-charmed. "Chasing small insects, such as grasshoppers and butterflies" was when a loud klaxon started blaring that the Whimsy Pipe would soon be descending. Up until the end, I was waiting for some kind of twist or wrinkle (although props where they're due for not going with "what if fun thing… was Cthulhu?"), but nope, it was goofy all the way down. Not my bag, but it made a lot of peoples' days. Still, -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Collecting as many dogs as possible and attempting to use them as currency to purchase more dogs,

+9001

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeeeeasy +1, one of my new favorite reads on the site.
Onward, and upward. Moon Champion soars!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God bless Moon Champion and his brother

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Challenging a fire hydrant to single combat, which it then destroyed via punching

wonderful

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"MOON CHAMPION, champion of the MOON!" has now become a running joke with myself and my friends.

I would love to see what happens if Moon Champion and Dr. Spanko try to talk to each other. It would be incredible.

Hilariously bizarre. Though I also liked how there were a couple parts that felt a bit more ominous

Its phenomena are disgusting and incredible.

Time waits for no Moon Champion. I have asked it nicely. It did not listen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so incredible. It's a full on comedy article, not just humorous like the Abyssal Typewriter, but it's still a very solid anomalous object that doesn't feel out of place at all.

You might disagree that this is a comedy article but the LUNAtic is some sort of comedic character archetype. It's very dangerous and uncontainable, but all of this comes from the comical characteristics. It also challenges the Foundation's mission to preserve normalcy. I loved it.

item.title: Re: MOON CHAMPION, AWAAAAAAAAAAAY
item.content:encoded: Is there any particular reason the image should be mirrored?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Such a doofus. Reminds me of Henry Kuttner's Hogben stories.
Upvoted and translated enthusiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the moon has its very own Don Quixote! Fantastic! I like to think that Harry Godsall is alive and well, and is now Moon Champion's very own Sancho Panza, and that they'll both be back soon to attack some wind farms!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sure someone much better than me has beaten me to it, but I made a reading of this SCP, and I kinda like what I did for Moon Champion's lines. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love moon champion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GODSPEED, MOON CHAMPION!!!!
This is so wonderfully charming and silly. I enjoyed every word of it! I hope he and his brother are getting on well in their epic battles against the terrors of sky and sea.

i absolutely adore this <3 +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let us away, fair meatfellow!

I will now end all social interactions this way.
Refer all complaints to CadaverCommander.

Did anyone else start hearing our fair champion in the voice of Invader Zim?

+1 and let us away, fair meatfellow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, 32-year-old beardy hipster from the east coast? He Knows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I was linked to this while getting critique for a similar "illogical humanoid" SCP I'm writing, and…good lord I love this. I'm not even jealous that you did the concept so well, if anything it makes me want to tweak my WIP a bit to give this beauty more space to shine.

I know that a big part of SCPs like this for a lot of people is how sheerly enigmatic they are, and they delight in the Sisyphean task of attempting to make sense of them, so not to ruin the magic for anyone,
buuuuuuut...
buuuuuuut...

I can't help but see the Moon Champion as a player character in an open-world game, perhaps even one who's godmodded themself (hell, the way it escape's Earth's orbit reminds me of noclipping to the top of the map). The list of observed behaviors especially reminds me of like, a goofy-ass "let's play" (like the time my friend Charley and I streamed Skyrim and decided all we'd do in the game was collect cheese).

This rules. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, why don't we have more fanart of this brilliant bastard.

Absolutely +1, made my morning.
HUZZAH, FOR MOON CHAMPION, CHAMPION OF THE MOON, HAS CONQUERED EVIL

item.title: Nope
item.content:encoded:
You are wrong here on so many levels.

"Gasses are fluid by very definition. Air's resistance to move out of the way of a high velocity object results in friction."

Not true. (although they do act similar to fluids) it's due to a combination of the viscosity and density of the gas.

+ show block
– hide block

"What you have just said is akin to saying that something was not shot, but instead that a directed explosion propelled a metal object towards a target."

Once again incorrect. It's like saying it's been shot, not through magnetic propulsion, but through a directed explosion.

"Air resistance is a type of friction."

No, it's not. Air resistance is caused by air pushing back against you. (due to every action having an equal and opposite reaction) which is in turn, is affected by the viscosity and density of the air you are moving through. (similar to how it is easier to swirl your finger through water than honey)

"High velocity objects cause air to compress in front of them thanks to inertia. This, in turn, causes friction, which causes significant heat to be released and often causes damage to said high velocity object."

Still no. Well, the inertia thing is kinda right, but the rest? REALLY far of. When you compress a gas it heats up, the opposite is also true. (that's how AC/Fridges work if you're curious)
Examples of this:

https://physics.stackexchange.com/questions/227230/compression-vs-friction-what-happens-when-space-objects-enter-earths-atmosph

https://home.howstuffworks.com/ac1.htm

https://www.realsimple.com/food-recipes/tools-products/appliances/how-does-refrigerator-work


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

Also, this video/wiki is of a guy starting a fire through air compression: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SkWJdWGdgaM
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fire_piston
-reentering the atmosphere is like this on steroids.
Also, this is how diesel engines work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Friction is the force resisting the relative motion of solid surfaces, fluid layers, and material elements sliding against each other. Gasses are fluid by very definition. Air's resistance to move out of the way of a high velocity object results in friction. What you have just said is akin to saying that something was not shot, but instead that a directed explosion propelled a metal object towards a target. They are the same thing, just in fewer words. Air resistance is a type of friction. High velocity objects cause air to compress in front of them thanks to inertia. This, in turn, causes friction, which causes significant heat to be released and often causes damage to said high velocity object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool SCP, got to point out though, that objects burn up as the inter the atmosphere due to the air compressing, not fiction.

https://www.thenakedscientists.com/articles/questions/why-burn-entering-earths-atmosphere
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atmospheric_entry
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aerodynamic_heating (this one says both, but the vast majority of it comes from compression)

item.title: Re: Dumber non-crossover speculation.
item.content:encoded:
…

¿Por qué no los dos?

item.title: Re: Dumber non-crossover speculation.
item.content:encoded: There is an Earth Champion and it is now and has been Gunhead, Slayer of Trilobites for the last 500 million years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You lunartic fuckin' nutter.

+1

item.title: Dumb fanfic crossover speculation.
item.content:encoded: There IS an Earth Champion. And his name is Finn Mertens.

item.title: Re: MOON CHAMPION, AWAAAAAAAAAAAY
item.content:encoded:
Greatest thus far? No, you this entire website has officially peaked. Pack your shit, it's over, we're going home. And by home I mean MOON.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is beautiful. May the Moon Champion eternally survive to serve the great Moon Kingdom in his quest for JUSTICE against the evil Moon Monsters.
+1 IN THE NAME OF JUSTICE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im getting distinct "Kaldor Draigo From TTS" vibes. I like this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the funniest SCPs out there, you gotta love all of Moon Champion's quirks and speeches. Definitely a +1 from me, and salutations, Moon Champion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Collecting as many dogs as possible and attempting to use them as currency to purchase more dogs

This was the line that sent my sides to join Moon Champion in his crusade for SPACE JUSTICE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god, Flash Gordon meets Mighty Morphin' Power Rangers is the vibe I get from this (also maybe a little of The Tick and Adam West being Catman or Mayor West) and just, so much yes to that. I think it might be the funniest thing I've seen all year as well.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a lot of trouble believing that a 32 year old business owner from rural New York has any idea what cosplay is.

I would be surprised if they didn't, honestly. 32-year-olds these days aren't usually super disconnected from pop culture, even those out in the sticks. Who's to say he doesn't know people who have gone to NYCC? I dunno, I just think it's funny that out of everything going on in this article, this is the thing that stretches the imagation too far. It's not like we're talking about Hupert Guthrie from a retirement home in Abilene Texas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finished reading, this article is great and I wish I could vote more than once! That tiny nitpick is the only flaw I could perceive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not done yet (like what I've read so far), but this stuck out to me:

Holy crap dude, it's like… 90 degrees out there. I get being dedicated to your cosplay and all that, but goddamn

I have a lot of trouble believing that a 32 year old business owner from rural New York has any idea what cosplay is. I feeling like "playing dressup" or just "costumes" would be better words than "cosplay". Nitpick, yes, but it really took me out of the story for a second because it felt like authorial voice leaking through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Knocking it out of the park once again woth yet another fun and humorous character. We need to just clone you already.

Congrats~! I would like to mention that I did not laugh while reading this. It might be because I am sick, and I definitely found myself smiling, but I did not laugh. I mention this not to put you, dear author, down. I mention this because there's enough oddness here to make me feel possibly a little uncomfortable. Just ever so mildly disturbed. +1

Also is he the "lunatic" because he is "lunar"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you tell me who else is on this team

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everything about this. Is fucking amazing. +1 no issues here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing. This is a perfect example at how something wacky and utterly shameless can portay how fucked up the Universe actually is by allowing it to exist. The upvote buttom doesn't let me vote enough times. +Moonvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the "Examples of observed detour behaviors include:" bullet point list is the best thing I've read all week. I started smiling at "Chasing small insects, such as grasshoppers and butterflies", and kept smiling through the rest of the list, and snickered out loud at "Marching directly into bodies of water such as ponds and lakes".

Good show here! Hearty +1 from me, since I love these kinds of articles. In my headcanon, these sorts of weird, quirky, but unmistakably anomalous entities are the ones that the Foundation encounter most often. I love the whole world, it's such a brilliant place, boom de yada, etc.

Also, Moon Champion and Moon Wizard adventures when

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I died when Moon Champion walked through the bakery wall.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is unfortunately pretty similar to 2622, but with a Moon man instead of a mole man. That said, the interactions were entertaining enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got nothin.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: moon champion and moon companion!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1233: May you and your people remain moist and rubbery.

[Insert dirty joke here]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is everything that I hoped it would be when you told me about it. absolutely fantastic read. I love him and I hope he finds a puppy capable of withstanding a vacuum

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Between Moon Champion and Cactus Man, I'd say we need about 2-3 more of funny superheroes so we can have the SCP Justice Avengers or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Collecting as many dogs as possible and attempting to use them as currency to purchase more dogs

*ok-hand emoji*

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm fucking dead. This is amazing.

I love that classic bit of English sarcasm at the end that results in the dude's demise. Or maybe he got turned into another Moon Champion?

Take my upvote you madman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: implying Godsall died

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: great. honestly, one of those rare articles where the length is justified by the content within. very funny without being too "wacky" and just a little tidbit of morbid humor at the end to make it moderately disturbing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MOON CHAMPION SOARS ETERNAL, ON WINGS OF JUSTICE

and a jetpack

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WHAT YOU ACTUALLY FUCKING DID IT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Greetings, little girl. I am Moon Champion, champion of the Moon, defender of space justice and destroyer of evil.

Sold, instant +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, little girl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
took your suggestion and carved some meat off the power wank at the start.

thankee much. and may Justice shine upon you always, noble warrior. o7

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I too would probably join your fight against the Moon Monsters, brave Champion.

There are few things that I read here where I am actually jealous that I didn't write it, almost all of them by CadaverCommander, this too is one.
